name: Suzy's Lime Cheesecake
recipeIngredient: Green food coloring - a few drops
recipeInstructions:
A Note from Suzy: As we do not use RAW/UNcooked eggs here in the northeastern United States due to salmonella bacteria I have substituted a product called "Just Whites", it works just as well if not better than egg whites.
CRUST: Combine coconut, flour and melted butter. Press onto bottom of a 9-inch springform pan. Bake at 350°F for 12-15 minutes until lightly browned. Remove from oven and set aside.
FILLING: Soften gelatin in 1/4 cup cold water. Set aside. In a saucepan combine egg yolks, 3/4 cup water, and 3/4 cup sugar. Stir over medium heat for 5 minutes. Remove from heat and add softened gelatin and stir until dissolved. Beat cream cheese at low speed while gradually adding gelatin/egg mixture until well blended. Stir in lime juice, grated lime rind, and a few drops of green food coloring. Fold in whipped cream and stiffly beaten egg whites. Pour filling over cooled crust and chill until firm. Remove from springform pan and garnish with grated lime rind. In summer, decorate serving plate with fresh mint leaves if desired.
